classificationRules.justification: Root CI shell entrypoints construct, test, compare, and publish protected artifact evidence under the canonical CI authorities.
The root flake is an explicit reviewed fingerprint authority for canonical artifact construction.
The reviewed root Buck environment and pinned shim are canonical Buck execution authorities for artifact commands.
The reviewed direnv entrypoint selects the developer shell without constructing or publishing repository artifacts.
The reviewed pre-commit hook enters the tool shell to apply and restage explicit source formatting and lint reconciliation.
The public build front door exposes the reviewed diagnostic-only impure switch before canonical artifact admission.
Public artifact and pinned Nix front doors enforce the canonical artifact environment before construction.
The public development launcher is the explicit live working-tree execution route.
Public maintenance, dependency, patch, scaffold, and update front doors own explicit reconciliation or mutation.
Remaining reviewed public command aliases coordinate developer tools, verification, or deployment without constructing repository artifacts.
The reviewed local uv2nix flake is imported by the canonical per-system Nix context and constructs Python artifacts.
Root bootstrap and initialization entrypoints own explicit workspace installation and reconciliation.
Live development launchers and watcher coordinators intentionally retain working-tree observation and cannot publish artifacts.
The explicit development-only diagnostic switch is parsed and materialized only at these exact command boundary files.
The public build command and its private orchestration implement canonical artifact admission and construction, including explicitly classified non-release development bundles.
The shared Nix action helper keeps an explicit diagnostic-only impure switch; normal artifact callers pass false.
Explicit update, install, bootstrap, scaffolding, repair, and maintenance commands own mutation or reconciliation.
Language artifact rules must use canonical immutable inputs, tools, and artifact policy.
Shared Buck artifact actions, wiring, provider metadata, and command assembly are canonical artifact boundaries.
Migration bundles are declared Buck artifacts and must use canonical action inputs and tools.
Nix derivations and generated scripts are artifact construction boundaries.
Local, CI, remote, and Buck artifact executors share canonical environment, source, and policy authorities.
Protected artifact runtime verification, aggregate retrieval, and signing execute Nix only through the canonical immutable evidence authorities.
Deployment control-plane commands consume reviewed build outputs and manage external state; they do not construct repository build artifacts.
Buck graph export and provider synchronization commands produce control-plane metadata rather than build artifacts.
Verification, status, cleanup, inventory, and developer control commands inspect or coordinate state without publishing artifacts.
These shared utilities support bootstrap, inspection, querying, deployment tests, and process control without constructing artifacts.
